item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Full Licensing Information:

The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: 1305
Author(s): Roget, Elogee FishTruck
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by Elogee FishTruck as a grayscaled version of an image by Roget
License: CC BY-SA 3.0

item.title: Image Credit
item.content:encoded: I took this picture, Licensehower did the manipulation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ve always thought I could prune this one up, I’ll look into it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one lacks the details I personally like to see with creepy biological scips. A great example being the fact that this critter devours all that biomass and does nothing with it. Why not have it leave behind a fresh version of itself? A regretful -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The entries at the end turn this from a plain ol freaky SCP to something that genuinely terrified me

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a chance to speak with Roget about this nifty article while I was doing some crosslink research (thats a real thing, shut up) and according to them they headcanon a connection between the Cat lure and SCP-1513, given both anomalies ability to mimic household domestic pets. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

When active, SCP-1305 will attempt to hide itself in a dark, enclosed area, such as a cupboard or closet. When it has hidden itself sufficiently, it may release a "lure" at the end of its tongue, most commonly resembling a domesticated animal such as a domestic cat. This lure will usually display behaviors expected of the organism it is imitating, and attempt to bring human subjects to SCP-1305.

To this:

When active, SCP-1305 will attempt to hide itself in a dark, enclosed area, such as a cupboard or closet. When it has hidden itself sufficiently, it may release a "lure" at the end of its tongue, most commonly resembling a domesticated animal such as a domestic cat1.This lure will usually display behaviors expected of the organism it is imitating, and attempt to bring human subjects to SCP-1305.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Similarity between SCP-1305's mimicry behaviors, and that of instances of SCP-1513 has been noted. Investigation into the possiblity of a shared origin for both anomalies is ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Nothing too scary, but the last bit gave me the willies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this, but couldn't stop thinking of the Spongebob movie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: cat lure, cat lure all alone in the world was a cat lure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely liked this one, though the end note pinched it for me. Lure is interesting, though I've seen the concept used before in things. Like the idea of it taking on appearance of past meals. That and the fact that it moves on, which implies something to me, but I'm not sure what..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll fix it eventually

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ROGEEEEEEET THIS SCP STILL MAKES NO DAMN SENSE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In most cases, when a subject comes within 15 meters of the lure, it will revert to the shape of a prehensile tongue, wrapping around the subject's body.

15 meters is 50 feet; unless the tongue is really long, this doesn't make much sense. What's the point of having it be a lure if it'll just turn into a tongue when you get within 50 feet of it? Wouldn't, I dunno, 5 (1.5 meters) feet make more sense?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Had a nightmare where this happened…. upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted for potential but you should reeeeeeally fix all the other problems above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm kinda stuck on the creepypasta-ish nature of this one— on one hand, I like it. It's basic and somewhat charming. On the other hand, it's almost too simple. What it would really need to cross the line from meh to good is an unsettling log/final document. As is, this recovered document is a bit bland and uber-predictable. I'd upvote if it was actually, well— creepy.

edit: I'm thinking the specificity of the effect— the whole tongue deal— also hurts it. It'd be cool if the reveal was that the shape IS a lure, but we don't really know what happens to the victim, we just find X.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was feeling pretty meh about this.
Until I read those documents.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a not particularly well thought out devilfish that uses simulacra of people and animals as lures? Meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This process will continue until no prey organisms are left within the area, at which point SCP-1305 will sever itself from its added mass, and move to a new area.

Seems silly… So it goes through all this eating, makes lures out of what it eats, then just leaves what it ate behind to move on and do it again? There's not any sort of interesting hint at why it would do something that is clearly not for survival. You make it sound like an insect running purely on instinct but this just doesn't follow. The kid lure waving is kinda creepy but that's about all that's good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't too crazy about the whole insectoid aspect to it, as I feel like something more mammalian would fit with these features. I also agree with a lot that's been said up above about the 15m range and the 1/16th shrinking (or shrinking at all, as I think its size would be a neat obstacle for it to have to overcome), but I don't have a problem with its initial size being 150kg. As for its eating, I think if it's going to eat so much, you should elaborate more beyond having it gain more lures and 'detach itself from its additional mass', which is really vague when you don't clarify what that mass is (the lures? body fat? I don't even think insects gain body weight like that), or how it detaches from it. I also have to agree with Anax on the whole human body thing. If it's going to impersonate a person, I think it should at least sockpuppet the body with its tongue or something, rather than completely remake it after digestion.

The journal entries are also kind of narmy and expected. I mean, I've tried making journals before, and never would I bother to use ellipses in said journal. That's so much wasted effort for a sentiment/tone I'd be able to recognize myself.

I'm going to neutral vote because I like the direction its taking, but I'd really like you to try revising it a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed, especially the journal entries. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After some consideration, I've removed the last note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, almost everyone on the site (myself included at times) fails to realize just how large a meter really is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's certainly an interesting interpretation of that picture, but the journal's author taking the time to write down HOLD ON SON I'M COMING before going to find his son shattered my suspension of disbelief. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All of these are my problems, plus it really did not feel like anything new was presented here. The journal entries utterly failed to make me give a shit about the narrator, too.

I really want to stress that 15 meters is 50 feet. If it's going to eat anything that gets within fifty feet, what's the point of having a lure? Enough people are going to get close to it by sheer chance if it's anything approaching a decently sized area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe that's why it was "insectiod" instead?

(BTW, fixed now)

item.title: CREDIT WHERE IT'S DUE
item.content:encoded: Wilt gave me this idea awhile ago, but I couldn't think of a good way to write it until now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except that it's implied it does eat its prey whole. And 330 is on the low end for even being interested in stuff our size. Remember, large creatures tend to be rather rare in nature. Even if did, there's still a bunch of other issues. Like how this thing seems confused as to whether or not its an ambush predator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a pretty common trope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this is pretty terrifying. (I feel like I've seen something similar, using person-shaped lures. Maybe it wasn't around here, I'm not sure.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
330 pounds is big enough to consume most humans, esp. if it doesn't NEED to eat its prey whole.

(also this was terrifying, even if the journal entries seem a bit stilted at times… *throws an upvote at it and scurries off*)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How on earth is something that small able to eat someone? 150 kg is very little, there's many humans bigger than that. Also, 15 meters is a gigantic distance from the lure. Plus, how does it even sense when people come near the lure? Insects don't technically have tongues, and it's unclear how this "tongue" is even strong enough to pull in a whole human. What's the mechanism for shaping the lure, and with enough precision (Including clothes!) to fool someone who knows the mimicked individual intimately? I don't buy that this thing can squish itself down to about 6 inches high, much less eat someone in that position. If this thing actually catches something, it's getting about half its mass in food. This doesn't seem like it would suggest the thing constantly trying to eat more and more.

Yes, you can explain all this with "Well, it's anomalous!". But there's an awful lot of anomalies here, then. Enough that I can't buy this thing at all. It just doesn't make sense. On top of that, the journal entries are cliched and obvious, which would probably be enough for a downvote there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking "Wow this could be terrifying if done well"

And then those journal entries…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made some edits, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it properly be described as "insectoid" when it's got four legs? I think there might be a better term the Foundation would use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. Can't say this hugely impressed me. I mean, there's nothing really wrong with any of it, I just didn't find any of it all that interesting.
